corpus_building.implemented_as: The TXT paper is converted into a section-aware annotated mini-corpus.

tokenization.implemented_as: Every concept phrase and sentence is normalized into tokens for counts and n-grams.

normalization.implemented_as: Lower-casing and punctuation cleanup are used for phrase matching and deduplication.

n_gram.implemented_as: Concept length and document top unigrams/bigrams/trigrams are exposed.

bow_and_naive_bayes.implemented_as: Section BoW hits act as class-conditional section evidence for intro/method/experiment/conclusion style decisions.

tf_idf_document_term_matrix.implemented_as: Document-level frequency and TF-IDF matrices expose which section concepts are characteristic of this paper.

laplace_smoothing.implemented_as: P(section | concept) uses add-one smoothing, so unseen section-concept pairs are not forced to zero.

evidence_cue_lexicon.implemented_as: Cue phrases such as propose, achieve, outperform, and BLEU give transparent rule-based evidence hints.

sequence_labeling.implemented_as: The first model contributes BIO boundary evidence for concept phrase extraction.

canonicalization_noisy_channel_style.implemented_as: Observed surface phrases are mapped to canonical terms with an alias confidence score.

top_candidates.sentence: Although the base MobileNet architecture is already small and low latency, many times a specific use case or application may require the model to be smaller and faster.
In order to construct these smaller and less computationally expensive models we introduce a very simple parameter α called width multiplier.
Table shows the accuracy, computation and size trade offs of shrinking the MobileNet architecture with the width multiplier α.
Accuracy drops off smoothly until the architecture is made too small at α = 0.25.
We present a class of efficient models called MobileNets for mobile and embedded vision applications.
MobileNets are based on a streamlined architecture that uses depthwise separable convolutions to build light weight deep neural networks.
We introduce two simple global hyperparameters that efficiently trade off between latency and accuracy.
We introduce two simple global hyperparameters that efficiently trade off between latency and accuracy.
We present extensive experiments on resource and accuracy tradeoffs and show strong performance compared to other popular models on ImageNet classification.
We present extensive experiments on resource and accuracy tradeoffs and show strong performance compared to other popular models on ImageNet classification.
Convolutional neural networks have become ubiquitous in computer vision ever since AlexNet popularized deep convolutional neural networks by winning the Ima-geNet Challenge: ILSVRC 2012 .
The general trend has been to make deeper and more complicated networks in order to achieve higher accuracy .
However, these advances to improve accuracy are not necessarily making networks more efficient with respect to size and speed.

This paper describes an efficient network architecture and a set of two hyper-parameters in order to build very small, low latency models that can be easily matched to the design requirements for mobile and embedded vision applications.

This paper describes an efficient network architecture and a set of two hyper-parameters in order to build very small, low latency models that can be easily matched to the design requirements for mobile and embedded vision applications.
Section 3 describes the MobileNet architecture and two hyper-parameters width multiplier and resolution multiplier to define smaller and more efficient MobileNets.
Another small network is Squeezenet which uses a bottleneck approach to design a very small network.
Width multiplier can be applied to any model structure to define a new smaller model with a reasonable accuracy, latency and size trade off.
Table shows the accuracy, computation and size trade offs of shrinking the MobileNet architecture with the width multiplier α.
Accuracy drops off smoothly until the architecture is made too small at α = 0.25.
Table shows the accuracy, computation and size trade offs for different resolution multipliers by training Mo-bileNets with reduced input resolutions.
Figure shows the trade off between ImageNet Accuracy and computation for the 16 models made from the cross product of width multiplier α ∈ {1, 0.75, 0.5, 0.25} and resolutions {224, 192, 160, 128}.

Figure shows the trade off between ImageNet Accuracy and number of parameters for the 16 models made from the cross product of width multiplier α ∈ {1, 0.75, 0.5, 0.25} and resolutions {224, 192, 160, 128}.
We proposed a new model architecture called Mo-bileNets based on depthwise separable convolutions.
We investigated some of the important design decisions leading to an efficient model.
